figures.caption: Table 3. Hyperparameter sensitivity analysis on ECG dataset. All configurations use E2-CRF with default settings.
Figure 3. Spectral density comparison: training (left) vs. generated baseline vs. E2-CRF (right). E2-CRF matches baseline spectra while giving ∼2.2× speedup.

Table 2. Ablation study results on ECG dataset. Speedup is measured relative to baseline (no caching). Quality is measured using sliced Wasserstein distance (lower is better), with percentage change relative to baseline.

Figure 2. Visual comparison of generated samples from baseline frequency domain diffusion (middle column) and E2-CRF cached diffusion (right column) across multiple datasets. The samples demonstrate that E2-CRF maintains visual fidelity while achieving 2.2× speedup.

Table 1. Sliced Wasserstein distances (↓) evaluated in the time domain (SW (Dtrain,Sfreq), SW (Dtrain,Scache)) and in the frequency domain (SW (D̃train, S̃freq), SW (D̃train, S̃cache)) comparing baseline frequency domain diffusion and E2-CRF cached diffusion. For each distance, we report its mean ± 2 standard errors.
